LT2R-DY2: Catharina Elisabetha Thomas was born in 1680 in Solingen, in the Rhineland of Prussia. She later lived and died in Schifferstadt, near Ludwigshafen in the Rhineland-Palatinate, passing away in 1724.
L8M1-JZ8: Love Harris was born in 1673 in Suffolk, in the Nansemond region of colonial Virginia. Over the course of his life he moved south into the Carolina country, and he died in 1732 in Bertie County, North Carolina.
9MJ2-181: Sarah Roberts was born in 1690 in Boston, in the Massachusetts Bay Colony, and spent her earliest years there, with records placing her in the town through 1693. As an adult she made her way south to Virginia, where she died in 1729.
GXCL-5T5: John Jackson Griffeth, known in his day with the title "Sr.," was born in 1658 in the Isle of Wight County of colonial Virginia. He lived through the early decades of English settlement in the region before relocating to North Carolina, where he died in 1726 in Bertie County.
LK1K-J3G: Andrew Fletcher of Saltoun was born in 1655 and grew up at Saltoun Hall in Haddingtonshire, Scotland, where he served as Commissioner for Supply for the county from 1678 to 1683. A restless figure of his age, he spent years abroad in the Netherlands and Hungary and was present at Lyme Regis, Dorset, in June 1685. Returning to Scottish public life, he served as Commissioner for East Lothian from 1703 to 1707, and after a final residence at Saltoun Hall he died in London in 1716.
GHW7-LFG: Jean Baptiste Haimon I was born in 1665 in Boston, in the Massachusetts Bay Colony, and was remembered as one of the "Irlandais de la Nouvelle Angleterre," an Irishman of New England. He worked as a cordier, or ropemaker, and by 1712 was living in Quebec City in New France, where he died in 1715.
LVP5-873: Benjamin Moore Sr was born in 1640 in Salem, in the Massachusetts Bay Colony, and made his living as a mariner, recorded in that trade at Southampton in the New York Colony in 1667. He died in 1690 in Southold, on Long Island, and was buried in the Old Burying Ground of the First Presbyterian Church there.
KNJG-JF2: John Hoosier was born in 1764 in Princess Anne, Virginia, and around 1810 relocated to Louisiana. He settled in St. Landry Parish, where census records placed him in 1820 and 1830, and he died in 1840 in Calcasieu, Louisiana.
LTH2-5QC: Elizabeth Hicks Ware was born in 1635 in Dublin, Ireland, though some records suggest an alternative birth in Leicestershire, England. She crossed the Atlantic and arrived in Virginia in 1662, settling in New Kent County, where she died in 1676.
L66N-ZKX: John Howe was born in 1682 in Marlborough, in the Massachusetts Bay Colony, and lived his entire life in that town, dying there in 1754.
G67F-8JH: Johann Wilhelm Grimm was baptized on 15 September 1747 in Kusel, in the Rhineland-Palatinate of Germany. His family remained in the Palatinate region, with a residence recorded in 1757.
L89J-1Q6: Catharina Koppenhoeffer was born in 1666 and baptized that January in Westheim, in the Württemberg region of Germany. She lived for a time at Plieningen near Stuttgart before settling at Raibach in Hesse, where she died in 1740.
LB66-NCW: Heinrich Hirtzel was born in 1619 and baptized that February in Pfäffikon, in the canton of Zürich, Switzerland. He later moved into German lands and died in 1663 at Reihen, near Sinsheim in Baden.
N476-TLW: Mary was born around 1620 in England and crossed the Atlantic to the Massachusetts Bay Colony. She died around 1698 in Marlborough, in Middlesex County, her passing dated only by the inventory taken of her estate.
K2BC-RD5: Sir John Brereton was born in 1591 at Brereton cum Smethwick in Cheshire, England, and was honored with a knighthood. He died young in 1629, predeceasing his own father, and so never inherited the title of Baron.
LH39-WFB: Thankful Weekes was born in 1712 in Falmouth, in the Massachusetts Bay Colony, and lived to the age of seventy-three. She eventually settled in the South, dying in 1785 in Duplin County, North Carolina.
GL4B-451: Martha Experience Nulistanidolvi was born in 1657 in the Massachusetts Bay Colony. She later moved south into the Connecticut Colony, where she died.
L19R-8LL: Susanna Elizabeth Cresson was born in 1640 in Rijswijk, in the Dutch province of Holland, and crossed the Atlantic to the New World. She married in 1658 at the Dutch Reformed Church in New York City, and in later life she was affiliated with the Philadelphia Monthly Meeting as a Quaker. She died in 1712 in New York City.
GLNB-4XJ: Josepha Anna Bader was born in 1683 and baptized that March in Berg, in the Ravensburg district of Württemberg, Germany. Like many of her countrymen, she emigrated to America, settling in Pennsylvania, where she died in 1750 in Delaware Township near Philadelphia.
GH24-1SF: Anthony Morris II was baptized on 24 July 1614 in Whitbourne, Herefordshire, England, and was also recorded under the names Anthony Morris and Anthonius III Morris. He lived in England throughout his life and died there in 1655.
G329-WVW: Johann Peter Kirsch was born in 1654 in Baumholder, in the Rhineland region of Germany. He worked as a Schneider, or tailor, and recorded in the trade at Kusel, and he died in 1715 back in Baumholder.
MJJ2-TTT: Suzanne Durand was born in 1653 in Montivilliers, in Normandy, France. She was one of the "Filles du Roi," the King's Daughters sponsored to settle New France, arriving from Normandy aboard the St-Louis out of Dieppe on 25 September 1667. She made her life in Canada and died in 1710 at Lavaltrie, in the Berthier region of Quebec.
LTQZ-47K: Mary Parmenter was born in 1610 in Little Yeldham, Essex, England, and crossed the Atlantic to the Massachusetts Bay Colony. She died in 1690 in Marlborough, in Middlesex County.
LZ62-8JL: Thomas Woodier was born in 1610 in Godalming, Surrey, England, and lived his entire life in that town, dying there in 1673.
LVG3-NJW: Bridget Fitts was born in 1614 in Tavistock, Devon, England, and emigrated across the Atlantic to the Massachusetts Bay Colony. She settled in Bradford, in Essex County, where she died in 1673 and her will was later probated.
MPJB-N33: John Lamp Kouns was born in 1780 in Greene, Virginia, and as a young man moved west to Greenup County, Kentucky, where census records placed him from 1810 through 1830. Around 1840 he relocated again to Boone County, Indiana, and he died there in 1851 in Rosston, in Union Township.
LTLD-529: Abraham Breland Sr was born in 1725 and migrated in 1768 from North Carolina to Beaufort, South Carolina. He settled in St. Peter's Parish, where he was recorded in 1800, and he died in 1805 in what is now Jasper County, South Carolina.
M94M-JQQ: Griffin Evans was born in 1592 in Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England. He crossed the Atlantic to the Caribbean and died in 1635 in Christ Church, Barbados.
